title: Anthropic says an AI security startup's logo looks too much like its own
excerpt: Anthropic is suing AI-enabled security firm Abnormal AI for trademark infringement, alleging that one of the startup's rebranded logos resembles its own.
content_html:
Sam Sabin

Tue, July 21, 2026 at 3:41 AM MDT 4 min read

Anthropic is suing AI-enabled security firm Abnormal AI for trademark infringement, alleging that one of the startup's rebranded logos resembles its own.

Why it matters: The case is an unusual move for Anthropic given Abnormal is also a customer.

  • Abnormal builds AI-powered cybersecurity software, and Anthropic has lately been expanding into the cybersecurity market.

The Anthropic logo (left) and Abnormal AI logo (right) in question in the lawsuit. Credit: Anthropic, Abnormal AI

State of play: Anthropic filed a lawsuit on July 1 against Abnormal AI for trademark infringement.

  • The security startup rebranded last year from Abnormal Security to Abnormal AI.

  • Anthropic argues in the complaint that the new "A" version of Abnormal's logo is too similar to its own and that the logo is likely sparking confusion among AI and cybersecurity customers.

  • In response, Abnormal AI CEO Evan Reiser wrote in a blog post on July 7 that the company designed its logo in April 2021, "just months after Anthropic was founded."

The other side: Anthropic told Axios its primary concern with the lawsuit is avoiding customer confusion and that it wants Abnormal to remove the "" from its smaller logo.

  • The company told Axios it viewed litigation as a last resort, saying that trademark owners risk weakening their rights if they fail to challenge branding they believe is confusingly similar.

Threat level: The lawsuit seeks Abnormal AI's profits attributable to the allegedly infringing logo, along with damages, court costs and attorneys' fees.

  • Anthropic is also asking the court to bar Abnormal from continuing to use the disputed logo and related branding. Anthropic says it is not challenging Abnormal's full wordmark or branding that predated the company's 2025 rebrand.

  • Anthropic also told Axios that the lawsuit is about protecting its trademarks, not going after Abnormal's business, and that its own trademark rights date back to 2021.

What they're saying: "Anthropic's case looks pretty strong based on the similarity of the logo and the fact they're in the same field," technology lawyer Denise Howell told Axios.

  • In his blog post Reiser argues that Abnormal is in a different market than Anthropic. "Anthropic builds general-purpose AI language models. Abnormal builds specialized behavioral AI models to understand enterprise behavior and stop cyber attacks," he writes.

  • "Trademark law is all about preventing confusion, and the chances of confusion seem pretty high here," Howell says, adding that Abnormal may have some arguments to make about the generic natures of letters and the term "AI."
content_text: Sam Sabin Tue, July 21, 2026 at 3:41 AM MDT 4 min read Illustration: Allie Carl/Axios Anthropic is suing AI-enabled security firm Abnormal AI for trademark infringement, alleging that one of the startup's rebranded logos resembles its own. Why it matters: The case is an unusual move for Anthropic given Abnormal is also a customer. Abnormal builds AI-powered cybersecurity software, and Anthropic has lately been expanding into the cybersecurity market. The Anthropic logo (left) and Abnormal AI logo (right) in question in the lawsuit. Credit: Anthropic, Abnormal AI State of play: Anthropic filed a lawsuit on July 1 against Abnormal AI for trademark infringement. The security startup rebranded last year from Abnormal Security to Abnormal AI. Anthropic argues in the complaint that the new "A" version of Abnormal's logo is too similar to its own and that the logo is likely sparking confusion among AI and cybersecurity customers. In response, Abnormal AI CEO Evan Reiser wrote in a blog post on July 7 that the company designed its logo in April 2021, "just months after Anthropic was founded." The other side: Anthropic told Axios its primary concern with the lawsuit is avoiding customer confusion and that it wants Abnormal to remove the "" from its smaller logo. The company told Axios it viewed litigation as a last resort, saying that trademark owners risk weakening their rights if they fail to challenge branding they believe is confusingly similar. Threat level: The lawsuit seeks Abnormal AI's profits attributable to the allegedly infringing logo, along with damages, court costs and attorneys' fees. Anthropic is also asking the court to bar Abnormal from continuing to use the disputed logo and related branding. Anthropic says it is not challenging Abnormal's full wordmark or branding that predated the company's 2025 rebrand. Anthropic also told Axios that the lawsuit is about protecting its trademarks, not going after Abnormal's business, and that its own trademark rights date back to 2021. What they're saying: "Anthropic's case looks pretty strong based on the similarity of the logo and the fact they're in the same field," technology lawyer Denise Howell told Axios. In his blog post Reiser argues that Abnormal is in a different market than Anthropic. "Anthropic builds general-purpose AI language models. Abnormal builds specialized behavioral AI models to understand enterprise behavior and stop cyber attacks," he writes. "Trademark law is all about preventing confusion, and the chances of confusion seem pretty high here," Howell says, adding that Abnormal may have some arguments to make about the generic natures of letters and the term "AI."

content_html_zh:
萨姆·萨宾

MDT 2026 年 7 月 21 日星期二凌晨 3:41 阅读时间 4 分钟

Anthropic 正在起诉人工智能安全公司 Abnormal AI 商标侵权，指控该初创公司的一个重新命名的徽标与其自己的徽标相似。

重要性：考虑到 Abnormal 也是 Anthropic 的客户，此案对于 Anthropic 来说是一个不寻常的举动。

  • Abnormal 开发了人工智能驱动的网络安全软件，而 Anthropic 最近一直在向网络安全市场扩张。

诉讼中涉及的人类标志（左）和异常人工智能标志（右）。 图片来源：人择、异常人工智能

进展情况：Anthropic 于 7 月 1 日对 Abnormal AI 提起商标侵权诉讼。

  • 这家安全初创公司去年从 Abnormal Security 更名为 Abnormal AI。
  • Anthropic 在投诉中辩称，Abnormal 的新“A”版本徽标与自己的徽标过于相似，该徽标可能会在人工智能和网络安全客户中引发混淆。
  • 对此，Abnormal AI 首席执行官 Evan Reiser 在 7 月 7 日的博文中写道，该公司于 2021 年 4 月设计了徽标，“就在 Anthropic 成立几个月后”。

另一方：Anthropic 告诉 Axios，其对诉讼的主要担忧是避免客户混淆，并希望 Abnormal 从其较小的徽标中删除“”。

  • 该公司告诉 Axios，它将诉讼视为最后的手段，并表示，如果商标所有者未能对他们认为令人困惑的相似品牌提出质疑，他们的权利可能会被削弱。

威胁级别：诉讼要求 Abnormal AI 因涉嫌侵权的标识获得利润，以及损害赔偿、法庭费用和律师费。

  • Anthropic 还请求法院禁止 Abnormal 继续使用有争议的徽标和相关品牌。 Anthropic 表示，它不会挑战 Abnormal 在 2025 年品牌重塑之前的完整文字标记或品牌。
  • Anthropic 还告诉 Axios，该诉讼是为了保护其商标，而不是针对 Abnormal 的业务，并且其自己的商标权可以追溯到 2021 年。

他们的说法：“基于徽标的相似性以及它们属于同一领域的事实，Anthropic 的案件看起来相当有力，”技术律师 Denise Howell 告诉 Axios。

  • Reiser 在他的博客文章中指出，Abnormal 与 Anthropic 处于不同的市场。 “Anthropic 构建通用人工智能语言模型。 Abnormal 构建了专门的行为人工智能模型来了解企业行为并阻止网络攻击，”他写道。
  • 豪威尔说：“商标法的目的就是防止混淆，而这里出现混淆的可能性似乎相当高。”他补充说，“异常”可能对字母和术语“人工智能”的通用性质有一些争论。
